meaning: n.(对少女的呢称)妮,姑娘,<俚>娼妓
examples: She swapped her plain suit for a girlie dress.
Both came in colors that were the antithesis of the pretty pinks of the recent "girlie" era.
Orenstein is at war with what she describes as our hyper-feminized girlie-girl consumer culture.
The dates are part of her first world tour since the Girlie Show in 1993.
There is a place for the girlie-girl and tomboy princess in our society, with lots of varieties in between.
